text: Decision: Move CI from CircleCI to GitHub Actions. Reason: org-wide runners, better cache controls, fewer flakey jobs. We will remove .circleci and standardize on GH Actions.
Explicit decision: Enable TypeScript strict mode across packages to catch defects earlier. We accept short-term friction for long-term stability.
We compared Playwright vs Cypress for e2e. Decision: choose Playwright for cross-browser parity and built-in trace viewer.
Team agreed to standardize on Conventional Commits. Goal: predictable changelogs and to unlock automated release tooling soon.
Database stance: Use PostgreSQL for local dev, staging, and production; keep SQLite for unit tests for speed. Knex env configs added.
Semantic-release dry run looked clean. Next step: define release rules and protect main with required checks. Tentative target: enable next week.
We sped up the pipeline by caching Playwright browsers. CodeQL still manual—plan to turn it on by default after we settle the noisy alerts.
Pino logging plus timing middleware improved insight into slow paths. Early metric: CLI startup down ~80ms after config memoization.
